response:
Phase one needs ten hours while phase two needs twenty
Phase three needs forty and phase four needs eighty
Phase five needs two hundred hours to finish
The sum of these phases equals three hundred ten
New time for each phase is one point five times old time
Total duration was twenty weeks before the delay
Multiply twenty by one point five to get new total
New duration becomes thirty weeks for all work
This calculation assumes no change in phase order
All phases now take longer due to issues found
The plan must shift to fit this new timeline
Resources will stretch thin under this extra load
Fortune favors those who adapt to sudden changes
We must adjust our schedule to meet this new goal
Every team must work harder to stay on track
The project manager will revise the whole plan
No delay should stop us from reaching success
Our focus remains on finishing the job right
We will keep moving forward despite the odds
The final result will show our true strength
